name: Admiral Gorshkov-class frigate
overview_md:
The Admiral Gorshkov-class (Project 22350) represents Russia's attempt to modernize its surface fleet with a multi-role frigate combining advanced sensors, versatile armament, and stealth features. These 4,500-ton frigates are designed as the backbone of Russia's blue-water navy, capable of anti-surface, anti-air, and anti-submarine warfare in contested environments.

Strategically, the Gorshkov-class fills the gap between Russia's aging Soviet-era destroyers and smaller corvettes, providing a modern platform for power projection and fleet escort duties. The design emphasizes reduced radar cross-section, advanced combat management systems, and flexibility through its UKSK vertical launch system that can accommodate various missile types from anti-ship Kalibr cruise missiles to Zircon hypersonics.

The class incorporates Russia's most advanced naval technologies, including the Poliment-Redut air defense system and Zaslon combat management system. However, production has been severely hampered by Western sanctions affecting propulsion systems and electronics, with only four hulls commissioned as of 2024 despite the program beginning in the early 2000s.

In the current threat environment, these frigates represent Russia's most capable surface combatants, regularly deployed to the Mediterranean and conducting long-range Kalibr strikes during the Syria and Ukraine conflicts. While individually capable, their small numbers and production delays limit their strategic impact compared to Western frigate programs like the Type 26 or FFG(X).

specs_json.propulsion: CODLAG: 1 × gas turbine M90FR, 2 × diesel generators, electric motor

armament_json.name: 3S-14 UKSK VLS
armament_json.notes: Can fire Kalibr, Oniks, Zircon missiles

variants_json.name: Project 22350
variants_json.key_changes: Initial production standard with German diesel engines and Western electronics where available

variants_json.name: Project 22350M
variants_json.key_changes: Enlarged design, 48-64 VLS cells, domestically sourced propulsion and electronics

news.title: Damen launches ‘Drone Carrier’ for the Portuguese Navy
news.snippet: Damen Shipyards Group has launched the NRP D. João II a Multi-Purpose Vessel (MPV) 10720 for the Portuguese Navy. The vessel entered the water at Damen Shipyards Galati in Romania on April 7. Damen press release The launch event took place in the presence of Vice Admiral Pires on behalf of the Portu

combat_history.event: Admiral Gorshkov conducted first operational deployment, demonstrating long-range cruise capability and integration with Syrian operations
combat_history.significance: Proved operational readiness and strategic reach of the class

combat_history.event: Admiral Gorshkov successfully tested Zircon hypersonic missile from UKSK VLS, achieving Mach 8+ speeds
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated platform's ability to deploy next-generation hypersonic weapons

combat_history.event: Admiral Gorshkov fired Kalibr cruise missiles at Ukrainian targets from the Barents Sea, demonstrating 1000+ km strike capability
combat_history.significance: First wartime use of the class, proving long-range precision strike capability

modernization.description: Replacing German MTU diesel engines with Russian alternatives due to sanctions, using NPO Saturn powerplants
modernization.impact: Reduces Western dependency but may affect reliability and fuel efficiency

modernization.description: Larger hull with 6000+ ton displacement, 48-64 VLS cells, improved sensors and longer range
modernization.impact: Would transform class into destroyer-equivalent capability

modernization.description: Replacing Western electronic components with Russian alternatives in navigation, communications, and combat systems
modernization.impact: Sanctions compliance but potentially reduced capability and reliability

vulnerabilities.description: Severe delays due to sanctions affecting propulsion and electronics supply chains. Original 8-year build time now extending to 12+ years per hull
vulnerabilities.context: Limits fleet expansion and operational availability when Russia needs modern surface combatants most
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Domestic substitution programs ongoing but with performance compromises

vulnerabilities.description: Only 16 UKSK cells limits simultaneous engagement capability compared to Western contemporaries with 32+ cells
vulnerabilities.context: In high-intensity conflict, would rapidly exhaust long-range strike missiles
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Project 22350M promises increased cell count but timeline uncertain

vulnerabilities.description: Low numbers force individual deployments rather than task group operations, reducing overall combat effectiveness and survivability
vulnerabilities.context: Cannot sustain simultaneous multi-theater operations or absorb combat losses

vulnerabilities.description: Advanced systems require sophisticated maintenance capabilities not widely available in Russian fleet
vulnerabilities.context: Operational availability rates may suffer, particularly for forward deployments
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Investment in shore infrastructure ongoing but resource-intensive

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Mediterranean patrol, Northern Fleet flagship duties, show-of-force deployments
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Usually operates independently or with support vessels, occasionally with Admiral Kuznetsov
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: High readiness when operational but limited numbers restrict simultaneous deployments

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Type 26 emphasizes ASW with quieter propulsion and better sonar, while Gorshkov prioritizes land-attack with more strike missiles. Type 26 has superior build quality but higher cost.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Modern frigate philosophies - stealth vs firepower, quality vs quantity

peer_comparisons.key_differences: FREMM has proven export success and NATO integration, while Gorshkov has more VLS cells and indigenous weapons. FREMM more reliable but less heavily armed.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: European cooperation vs Russian independence in naval design

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Constellation prioritizes air defense and networking while Gorshkov emphasizes strike warfare. US frigate has better sensors and datalinks but fewer offensive weapons.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Next-generation frigate designs - US distributed lethality vs Russian concentrated firepower

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Type 054A built in much larger numbers (40+ hulls) but less capable individually. Chinese frigate more reliable production but less advanced weapons and sensors than Gorshkov.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Russian precision vs Chinese mass production in naval modernization

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Gorshkov adopted some Sachsen air defense concepts but added strike warfare emphasis. German frigate more specialized for air defense, Russian more multi-role.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Technology transfer and adaptation - how Russian designs evolved from Western concepts

doctrine.role: Distributed sea control and expeditionary support within 1,000nm of Russian shores, providing credible surface combatant presence where Soviet-era destroyers are too valuable to risk and corvettes lack endurance.
doctrine.employment: Typically operates in task groups of 2-3 frigates with submarine support, conducting extended patrols in the Baltic, Black Sea, and Mediterranean. Primary missions include convoy escort, naval diplomacy, and forward area air defence for amphibious operations. Command relationship varies between Northern Fleet flag operations and regional fleet integration, with increasing emphasis on independent deployment capability to reduce dependence on fleet auxiliaries.
doctrine.design_philosophy: Prioritised sensor integration and VLS magazine depth over traditional Russian emphasis on large anti-ship missiles, reflecting shift toward NATO-style engagement envelopes. Sacrificed some single-mission lethality (compared to Udaloy ASW focus) for multi-mission flexibility, accepting higher unit cost for reduced fleet logistics burden through improved reliability and maintainability.
doctrine.threat_context: Designed for high-intensity NATO naval opposition with integrated air-sea battle concepts, emphasising survival against coordinated air-surface missile attacks. The threat has evolved toward more sophisticated electronic warfare and hypersonic weapons that challenge the platform's air defence assumptions, while submarine threats have intensified in traditional Russian operating areas.
doctrine.comparison_frame: Compare primarily on VLS capacity versus displacement efficiency and sensor integration maturity, not raw speed or single-mission firepower. Both Western and Russian designers accept 29-30 knot maximums as sufficient, making magazine depth per ton and radar horizon management the decisive factors for sustained operations.

curated_sources.title: Modern Naval Combat by Milan Vego
curated_sources.relevance: Provides doctrinal framework for understanding Russian surface warfare concepts and multi-mission frigate employment in contested waters.

curated_sources.title: Russia's Military Revival by Bettina Renz (RUSI)
curated_sources.relevance: Essential context for understanding Project 22350 within broader Russian naval modernisation and strategic priorities.

curated_sources.title: Russian Navy: A Historic Transition (IISS Strategic Dossier)
curated_sources.relevance: Authoritative analysis of Russian naval platform development philosophy and the transition from Soviet-era design principles.

curated_sources.title: Michael Kofman (Russian Military Analysis)
curated_sources.relevance: Leading Western analyst of Russian military capabilities with frequent commentary on Project 22350 developments and operational deployment patterns.

curated_sources.title: Admiral Gorshkov Class - Naval Technology
curated_sources.relevance: Comprehensive technical specifications and construction timeline for understanding platform capabilities and program challenges.

curated_sources.title: Project 22350 Frigate - Seaforces Online
curated_sources.relevance: Detailed specifications database with sensor and weapons system integration details critical for capability assessment.

curated_sources.title: Russia's Naval Resurgence by James Goldrick (RUSI)
curated_sources.relevance: Strategic context for Project 22350 role in Russian naval power projection and Mediterranean operations.
